reviewText.description: The text of the product review to analyze

sentiment.description: The overall sentiment of the review

optimization.instruction:
## sentiment_analysis
- **Negative Sentiment Indicators**
• Strong negative phrases like "waste of money" indicate clear negative sentiment
• High confidence scores (0.9+) are appropriate for unambiguous negative language
• Response format should include both sentiment label and confidence score
- **Neutral Sentiment Indicators**
• Lukewarm expressions like "okay", "fine", "decent" indicate neutral sentiment
• Middle-ground opinions without strong emotional valence should be classified as neutral
• Confidence scores in the 0.7-0.8 range are appropriate for moderately ambiguous neutral cases
• Neutral classification applies when content lacks clear positive or negative emotional indicators
- **Compound Negative Sentiment**
• Multiple negative indicators in the same text (e.g., product defects + poor service) reinforce overall negative sentiment
• Compound negative cases justify high confidence scores (0.95+) due to multiple supporting evidence points
• Common compound patterns include: product quality issues + customer service problems, delivery issues + product defects
• Each additional negative factor strengthens the overall sentiment classification confidence
- **Recommendation-Based Negative Sentiment**
• Direct negative recommendations like "Would not recommend" or "Don't buy this" are strong negative sentiment indicators
• Recommendation language often carries high certainty and should receive confidence scores of 0.9+ 
• These expressions typically reflect overall dissatisfaction even without specific complaint details
• Negative recommendation statements are particularly valuable for sentiment analysis as they represent actionable consumer advice
- **Positive Sentiment Indicators**
• Strong positive adjectives like "Amazing", "Excellent", "Outstanding" indicate clear positive sentiment
• Expressions of satisfaction with specific service aspects (customer service, delivery, quality) signal positive sentiment
• High confidence scores (0.9+) are appropriate for unambiguous positive language
• Positive sentiment classification applies when content expresses clear satisfaction, praise, or approval
